summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #447 where IPv6 bind addresses caused unparseable socket address strings, then get the PR merged with clean CI.
summary.outcome: Fixed the IPv6 bug across all affected files, resolved all PR review comments, merged main into the branch handling a large structural refactor that moved gateway code to a new httpd crate, and completed a merge commit.

repo: Between the PR branch and main, gateway startup code moved from `crates/gateway/src/server.rs` to `crates/httpd/src/server.rs` — `start_gateway` no longer exists in the gateway crate.

The TLS crate uses a custom `Context` trait via `impl_context!()` macro (in its own error module), not `anyhow::Context` — it provides `with_context(|| ...)` with `FnOnce() -> C where C: Into<String>`.
The `crates/oauth/src/callback_server.rs` OAuth callback server has its own `Error` enum with no `CallbackServer` variant — use `Error::message()` for custom error strings.
The project uses nightly rustfmt (`nightly-2025-11-30`) with strict line-length formatting enforced in CI via `cargo +nightly fmt --check`.
CI runs local checks via a justfile recipe `format-check` (line 14) that includes biome, i18n, fmt, and zizmor checks in parallel.
The beads issue tracker DB was in a broken state on this worktree — beads commands are unreliable here.

code.finding: After main's refactor, the IPv6 bug (`format!("{bind}:{port}")`) lives here, not in gateway/src/server.rs.
HTTP redirect server bind address construction — was also using the buggy format pattern.
OAuth callback server bind — fixed to use IpAddr parse + SocketAddr::new; error message must use Error::message().

workflow: When resolving diff3-format merge conflicts manually, all three sections (HEAD, base `|||||||`, and incoming `>>>>>>>`) must be removed — leaving the base section orphaned causes silent duplicate code that compiles with errors.
For large structural merges where main rewrote the conflicted file, `git checkout main -- <file>` then reapplying the patch is cleaner than manual conflict resolution.
Greptile bot auto-comments on PRs with code review suggestions — all three initial comments were from it, not human reviewers.
PR review threads can be bulk-resolved via GraphQL `resolveReviewThread` mutation using node IDs fetched from the PR review threads query.

friction: The diff3 merge conflict left an orphaned `||||||| <base>` section after manual marker removal, causing ~700 lines of duplicate code and many compilation errors before being detected.
The first merge attempt had to be aborted (`git merge --abort`) after the orphaned base section caused compilation failures — required re-merging from scratch.
Multiple rounds of PR review comments (3 separate passes) required repeated fetch-fix-push-resolve cycles.
The beads issue tracker was non-functional on this worktree, blocking issue creation.
The nightly rustfmt required a line to be on one line (`let listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(...).await?`) that the editor had split — caught only in CI.
open_items: The `fe80::1` test case has a comment about zone ID requirement for actual bind — zone ID handling in IPv6 link-local addresses is not implemented.
Beads issue tracker DB is broken on the abalone-carrot worktree — needs investigation or re-initialization.
The merge commit was initiated but the transcript ends before confirming it completed and CI passed on the merged branch.
